$comment: Generative news-article corpus for the 2000s (digital live-timing and online race-report voice), parsed by Companion.Core.News.NewsArticleBank. Crisp modern newswire cadence uses official classifications, web desks, data review and paddock feeds without inventing sector, broadcast, component or manufacturer facts. Keys under 'bodies' are 'journalPhase|cause'; each holds era-keyed body templates selected deterministically. Bodies compose literal prose, fact tokens, and {pool:name} references into 'pools'. Fact tokens: {player} {team} {race} {year} {round} {position} {expected} {winner} {fieldSize} {champPosition} {champMove} {champLeader}. Add new eras/events by adding JSON only.

2000s: LIVE TIMING REPORT: {race}, {year}.
RACE CENTRE: {race}, round {round}.
FROM THE MEDIA CENTRE AT {race}.
ONLINE RACE REPORT: {race}, {year}.
PADDOCK WIRE: {race}, round {round}.

PRESS ROOM FEED: {race}, round {round}.

RACE DESK: {race}, round {round}.
OFFICIAL CLASSIFICATION: {race}, {year}.
PIT WALL BULLETIN: {race}, round {round}.

2000s: The engineers leave with a difficult result to review.

Questions for the debrief, and few easy answers.

The timing sheet travels home for a long night of analysis.

Straight into the debrief, and no shortage of questions.
The review list grows before the next round.
One for the 'lessons learned' folder on the server.
default: The team resets for the next round.
A day to file away and forget.
The team looks to the next round.

2000s: The season's record is archived; planning turns to next year.
The final standings are locked, and off-season work begins.
The championship site closes its live feed on a completed campaign.
The results archive is sealed and the winter review starts.
The standings are final, the updates stop, and planning begins again.
A season's worth of classifications goes into the archive.
The championship feed signs off until the next campaign.
The numbers are filed and the countdown to next year starts now.

The year is done; the next begins.
